task_text: Open project_helios_status.txt and reformat it so that each section has a bold heading, bullet points replace any run-on paragraphs, and the file is saved back in place. Do not change any of the factual content.

content.text: Let me start by reading the file to understand its current structure.

content.content:
**Project Helios — Weekly Status Update**
- Week of 2026-07-06

**Overall**
- ON TRACK (minor risks)

**Milestone Progress**
- Data ingestion module: 85% complete
- API gateway: 100% complete (delivered)
- Client dashboard: 60% complete
- Integration testing: 40% complete

**Sprint 14 Goals**
- Complete dashboard module v1
- Resolve data format inconsistencies (Blocker #1)
- Begin UAT preparation

**Open Blockers**
- 1. Meridian source data contains inconsistent date formats (ISO 8601 vs. MM/DD/YYYY).
  - Dev team awaiting clarification from Meridian IT. Estimated delay: 2-3 days.
- 2. Third-party mapping library (GeoSync v2.1) license renewal pending. Legal reviewing.
  - Target resolution: 2026-07-14.

**Next Milestone**
- Integration Test Sign-off — target 2026-07-25

content.text:
The file is already well-structured with bold headings and bullet points throughout. There are no run-on paragraphs — every section already uses proper formatting. The factual content is intact and clean. No reformatting changes are needed, and overwriting the file with identical content would be a no-op.

Here's a summary of what I found:

| Check | Status |
|---|---|
| **Bold headings** | ✅ All 6 sections already have `**Bold**` headings |
| **Bullet points** | ✅ Every item is already bulleted — no run-on paragraphs present |
| **Factual content** | ✅ Complete and accurate |

**The file is already correctly formatted.** Every section has a bold heading, and all content is expressed as bullet points (including the nested sub-bullets under Open Blockers). No changes were needed, so I've left the file untouched to avoid any unnecessary writes.

If you have a *different* version of this file, or if you'd like me to adjust the formatting style in any specific way (e.g., different heading markers, indentation style, spacing between sections), just let me know!
